topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of aged paper and strong coffee hung heavy in the air of our shared apartment. I found the laptop open on the kitchen table, the screen illuminating the harsh lines of his face. My breath hitched. It wasn't a work document; it was a manuscript. The title, stark and unforgiving, was my name. Every chapter, every meticulously crafted scene, detailed moments I hadn't even consciously registered—my quiet mornings, the way I chewed my lip when concentrating, the subtle shifts in my mood. A cold, hard knot tightened in my chest, squeezing the air from my lungs. I stared at the words, the ink mocking me with its intimacy. When he finally looked up, his eyes were wide with a kind of distant, almost clinical excitement. "It's coming together," he murmured, his voice smooth as polished stone. The silence that followed felt like a physical weight pressing down on my shoulders, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I pushed my chair back, the scrape of the legs against the floor sounding deafeningly loud.

[story 2]

I walked into the study late one Tuesday evening, drawn by the faint glow emanating from the desk lamp. What I saw made my stomach clench with a sudden, sharp resentment. Spread across the mahogany surface was a manuscript, pages filled with prose that charted my existence like a forensic report. He was hunched over it, a pen scratching furiously across the paper, his brow furrowed in intense concentration. Seeing it triggered a profound sense of being observed, dissected, reduced to mere narrative material. A tremor ran through my hands as I approached. "What is this?" My voice came out thin, stripped bare of warmth. He jumped, startled, quickly covering the document with a sheaf of papers. His reaction—that swift attempt to shield his creation—only sharpened the unpleasant feeling blooming in my gut. I didn't say anything, just stood there, my jaw tight, the muscles in my neck corded with tension. I needed to leave, needed to escape the suffocating proximity of this revelation.

[story 3]

The discovery happened accidentally, tucked inside a book he’d left on the bedside table. It was a draft, messy and raw, yet undeniably mine. Reading the first few paragraphs was like being struck. The narrative voice he’d adopted was so perfectly attuned to my idiosyncrasies, so devastatingly accurate in its portrayal of my internal landscape, that a strange, bitter heat rose in my throat. I picked up the manuscript, my fingers trembling slightly, and felt a wave of profound alienation wash over me. It felt like a betrayal, a theft of the most private corners of my being. When he walked in, whistling tunelessly, I didn't look up. I simply held the pages, letting the silence stretch until it became unbearable. When he finally noticed the tension radiating off me, his smile faltered, replaced by a look of bewildered defensiveness. I dropped the book onto the floor, the sound echoing the sudden, brittle fracture inside me.
